CheckResults.queryText:
/*********
CDM_TABLE
Verify the table exists.
Parameters used in this template:
schema = main
cdmTableName = PERSON
**********/
SELECT 
    num_violated_rows, 
    CASE 
        WHEN denominator.num_rows = 0 THEN 0 
        ELSE 1.0*num_violated_rows/denominator.num_rows 
    END AS pct_violated_rows, 
    denominator.num_rows AS num_denominator_rows
FROM
(
    SELECT 
        num_violated_rows 
    FROM
    (
        SELECT
            CASE 
                WHEN COUNT(*) = 0 THEN 0
                ELSE 0
            END AS num_violated_rows
        FROM main.PERSON cdmTable
    ) violated_rows
) violated_row_count,
( 
    SELECT 1 AS num_rows
) denominator
;
CheckResults.checkDescription: A yes or no value indicating if PERSON table is present as expected based on the specification.

CheckResults.queryText:
/*********
Table Level:  
MEASURE_OBSERVATION_PERIOD_OVERLAP
Determine what #/% of persons have overlapping or back-to-back observation periods
Parameters used in this template:
schema = main
cdmTableName = OBSERVATION_PERIOD
**********/
SELECT 
	num_violated_rows, 
	CASE 
		WHEN denominator.num_rows = 0 THEN 0 
		ELSE 1.0*num_violated_rows/denominator.num_rows 
	END AS pct_violated_rows, 
    denominator.num_rows AS num_denominator_rows
FROM
(
	SELECT 
		COUNT(violated_rows.person_id) AS num_violated_rows
	FROM
	(
		/*violatedRowsBegin*/
		SELECT DISTINCT
			cdmTable.person_id 
		FROM main.observation_period cdmTable
		JOIN main.observation_period cdmTable2 
		    ON cdmTable.person_id = cdmTable2.person_id
		    AND cdmTable.observation_period_id != cdmTable2.observation_period_id
		WHERE (cdmTable.observation_period_start_date <= cdmTable2.observation_period_end_date 
		    AND cdmTable.observation_period_end_date >= cdmTable2.observation_period_start_date)
		    OR ((cdmTable.observation_period_end_date + TO_DAYS(CAST(1 AS INTEGER))) = cdmTable2.observation_period_start_date)
		    OR ((cdmTable2.observation_period_end_date + TO_DAYS(CAST(1 AS INTEGER))) = cdmTable.observation_period_start_date)
		/*violatedRowsEnd*/
	) violated_rows
) violated_row_count,
( 
	SELECT 
		COUNT(DISTINCT cdmTable.person_id) AS num_rows
	FROM main.observation_period cdmTable
) denominator
;
CheckResults.checkDescription: The number and percent of persons that have overlapping or back-to-back observation periods.

CDM_FIELD
Verify the field exists.
Parameters used in this template:
schema = main
cdmTableName = COST
cdmFieldName = AMOUNT_ALLOWED
**********/
SELECT 
    num_violated_rows, 
    CASE 
        WHEN denominator.num_rows = 0 THEN 0 ELSE 1.0*num_violated_rows/denominator.num_rows 
    END AS pct_violated_rows, 
    denominator.num_rows AS num_denominator_rows
FROM (
    SELECT num_violated_rows FROM (
        SELECT
            CASE 
                WHEN COUNT(AMOUNT_ALLOWED) = 0 THEN 0
                ELSE 0
            END AS num_violated_rows
        FROM main.COST cdmTable
    ) violated_rows
) violated_row_count,
( 
    SELECT 1 AS num_rows
) denominator
;
CheckResults.checkDescription: A yes or no value indicating if AMOUNT_ALLOWED is present in the COST table as expected based on the specification.

schema = main

cdmFieldName = PERSON_ID
**********/
SELECT 
    num_violated_rows, 
    CASE 
        WHEN denominator.num_rows = 0 THEN 0 ELSE 1.0*num_violated_rows/denominator.num_rows 
    END AS pct_violated_rows, 
    denominator.num_rows AS num_denominator_rows
FROM (

        SELECT

                ELSE 0
            END AS num_violated_rows

) violated_row_count,

) denominator
;

CDM_FIELD
Verify the field exists.
Parameters used in this template:
schema = main
cdmTableName = PROVIDER
cdmFieldName = NPI
**********/
SELECT 
    num_violated_rows, 
    CASE 
        WHEN denominator.num_rows = 0 THEN 0 ELSE 1.0*num_violated_rows/denominator.num_rows 
    END AS pct_violated_rows, 
    denominator.num_rows AS num_denominator_rows
FROM (
    SELECT num_violated_rows FROM (
        SELECT
            CASE 
                WHEN COUNT(NPI) = 0 THEN 0
                ELSE 0
            END AS num_violated_rows
        FROM main.PROVIDER cdmTable
    ) violated_rows
) violated_row_count,
( 
    SELECT 1 AS num_rows
) denominator
;
CheckResults.checkDescription: A yes or no value indicating if NPI is present in the PROVIDER table as expected based on the specification.

CheckResults.queryText:
/*********
FIELD_CDM_DATATYPE
In some SQL dialects, check that integer fields only contain digits.
In others, check that integer fields are numeric and contain no decimal points
Parameters used in this template:
schema = main
cdmTableName = CARE_SITE
cdmFieldName = CARE_SITE_ID
**********/
SELECT 
	num_violated_rows, 
	CASE 
		WHEN denominator.num_rows = 0 THEN 0 
		ELSE 1.0*num_violated_rows/denominator.num_rows 
	END AS pct_violated_rows, 
	denominator.num_rows AS num_denominator_rows
FROM
(
	SELECT 
		COUNT(violated_rows.violating_field) AS num_violated_rows
	FROM
	(
		/*violatedRowsBegin*/
		SELECT 
			'CARE_SITE.CARE_SITE_ID' AS violating_field, 
			cdmTable.* 
		FROM main.CARE_SITE cdmTable
		WHERE 
			(CASE WHEN (CAST(cdmTable.CARE_SITE_ID AS VARCHAR) ~ '^([0-9]+\.?[0-9]*|\.[0-9]+)$') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END = 0 
			OR (CASE WHEN (CAST(cdmTable.CARE_SITE_ID AS VARCHAR) ~ '^([0-9]+\.?[0-9]*|\.[0-9]+)$') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END = 1 
				AND STRPOS(CAST(ABS(cdmTable.CARE_SITE_ID) AS varchar),'.') != 0))
			AND cdmTable.CARE_SITE_ID IS NOT NULL
		/*violatedRowsEnd*/
	) violated_rows
) violated_row_count,
( 
	SELECT 
		COUNT(*) AS num_rows
	FROM main.CARE_SITE
) denominator
;
CheckResults.checkDescription: A yes or no value indicating if the CARE_SITE_ID in the CARE_SITE is the expected data type based on the specification.  Only checks integer fields.

In some SQL dialects, check that integer fields only contain digits.
In others, check that integer fields are numeric and contain no decimal points
Parameters used in this template:
schema = main
cdmTableName = METADATA
cdmFieldName = METADATA_CONCEPT_ID
**********/
SELECT 
	num_violated_rows, 
	CASE 
		WHEN denominator.num_rows = 0 THEN 0 
		ELSE 1.0*num_violated_rows/denominator.num_rows 
	END AS pct_violated_rows, 
	denominator.num_rows AS num_denominator_rows
FROM
(
	SELECT 
		COUNT(violated_rows.violating_field) AS num_violated_rows
	FROM
	(
		/*violatedRowsBegin*/
		SELECT 
			'METADATA.METADATA_CONCEPT_ID' AS violating_field, 
			cdmTable.* 
		FROM main.METADATA cdmTable
		WHERE 
			(CASE WHEN (CAST(cdmTable.METADATA_CONCEPT_ID AS VARCHAR) ~ '^([0-9]+\.?[0-9]*|\.[0-9]+)$') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END = 0 
			OR (CASE WHEN (CAST(cdmTable.METADATA_CONCEPT_ID AS VARCHAR) ~ '^([0-9]+\.?[0-9]*|\.[0-9]+)$') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END = 1 
				AND STRPOS(CAST(ABS(cdmTable.METADATA_CONCEPT_ID) AS varchar),'.') != 0))
			AND cdmTable.METADATA_CONCEPT_ID IS NOT NULL
		/*violatedRowsEnd*/
	) violated_rows
) violated_row_count,
( 
	SELECT 
		COUNT(*) AS num_rows
	FROM main.METADATA
) denominator
;
CheckResults.checkDescription: A yes or no value indicating if the METADATA_CONCEPT_ID in the METADATA is the expected data type based on the specification.  Only checks integer fields.

            SELECT

            FROM main.PERSON
            GROUP BY PERSON_ID
            HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 
        )
        /*violatedRowsEnd*/

) violated_row_count,

) denominator

cdmFieldName = ADMITTED_FROM_CONCEPT_ID
fkTableName = CONCEPT
fkFieldName = CONCEPT_ID
**********/
SELECT num_violated_rows,
    CASE
        WHEN denominator.num_rows = 0 THEN 0 
        ELSE 1.0*num_violated_rows/denominator.num_rows
    END AS pct_violated_rows,
    denominator.num_rows AS num_denominator_rows
FROM (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(violated_rows.violating_field) AS num_violated_rows
    FROM (
        /*violatedRowsBegin*/
        SELECT 
            'VISIT_OCCURRENCE.ADMITTED_FROM_CONCEPT_ID' AS violating_field, 
            cdmTable.*
        FROM main.VISIT_OCCURRENCE cdmTable
            LEFT JOIN 
                main.CONCEPT fkTable
                ON cdmTable.ADMITTED_FROM_CONCEPT_ID = fkTable.CONCEPT_ID
        WHERE fkTable.CONCEPT_ID IS NULL 
            AND cdmTable.ADMITTED_FROM_CONCEPT_ID IS NOT NULL
        /*violatedRowsEnd*/
    ) violated_rows
) violated_row_count,
(
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS num_rows
    FROM main.VISIT_OCCURRENCE cdmTable
) denominator
;
CheckResults.checkDescription: The number and percent of records that have a value in the ADMITTED_FROM_CONCEPT_ID field in the VISIT_OCCURRENCE table that does not exist in the CONCEPT table.

Metadata.sourceDescription: Synthea version:  3.3.0   SyntheaTM is a Synthetic Patient Population Simulator. The goal is to output synthetic, realistic (but not real), patient data and associated health records in a variety of formats.
